mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or rolling 1-year IC drops below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stable headline inflation indicate healthy demand expansion without price pressures, supporting corporate margin growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL annualized volatility exceeds 2.5% over the preceding quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth leads equity markets in early-cycle recoveries as capacity utilization rises and supply constraints ease, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal flips to negative predictive power during supply-constrained inflationary regimes where production growth coincides with equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts reduces household wealth effects and construction activity, dragging down aggregate demand and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Housing declines do not correlate with equity drawdowns when fiscal stimulus or strong consumer credit growth offsets the demand loss.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening, which historically precedes wage-driven consumption growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative predictive power when wage growth decouples from employment gains, indicating productivity-driven rather than demand-driven labor dynamics.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly GDP growth accelerations directly expand the corporate earnings base, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between GDP growth acceleration and SPX forward returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns in contraction quarters are not significantly lower than expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations more severely than in low-inflation regimes due to real rate shocks and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when rate hikes are fully priced in and inflation is declining, or when real rates remain negative despite nominal hikes.
